blocks.text: Isabella Sandoval, Director of Operations, {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}}
Re: Facility Condition Assessment
Dear Ms. Sandoval:

This letter confirms that {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}} has engaged Torchlake Engineering Group LLC to perform a facility condition assessment of your main plant and warehouse. The deliverable is a deficiency register your team can budget against: every finding located, photographed, priced to a planning level, and ranked by consequence of deferral.
Scope of the assessment:

items: Building envelope, roof, and structure, walked floor by floor and documented against the record drawings you provide.
Mechanical, electrical, and plumbing systems, including remaining-useful-life estimates for major equipment.
Code and life-safety observations noted as found; we flag, your counsel interprets.

blocks.text: Jeff Fowler, Engineering Manager, directs the engagement and is your day-to-day contact. John Johnson, Project Engineer, leads the field walks and keeps the deficiency register. We will need roof and mechanical-room access, record drawings, and an escort for occupied areas.

Field work begins on {{fact:f:E-2019-001.start}}. Our fee for the full scope above is a fixed fee of {{fact:f:E-2019-001.fee}}, inclusive of travel within the region, invoiced in three equal installments. Scope changes are agreed in writing before we do the work.
We appreciate the opportunity. Please sign below and return one copy; keep the second for your files.
Sincerely,
To: Assessment team; cc Isabella Sandoval, Director of Operations, {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}}
From: Jeff Fowler, Engineering Manager
Re: Kickoff, Facility Condition Assessment
Field work starts {{fact:f:E-2019-001.start}}. Read this before the site day; the client walkthrough happens once and we do not get a second first impression.
Assignments:

items: 1. Envelope and structure: John Johnson leads the walks and owns the deficiency register from day one.
2. Mechanical and electrical: David Fowler documents equipment nameplates and remaining useful life; route anything ambiguous to Jennifer Mayo for review before it enters the register.
3. Site logistics and safety: Megan Thomas owns scheduling, escorts, and the safety plan filed with Ms. Sandoval's office.

blocks.text: Checklist discipline: photos named by room number the same day, nameplate data into the register before you leave site, and no finding without a location. If the two Fowlers are both on a call, use full names in notes; the register does not have a sense of humor.
Next steps: Megan circulates the site schedule this week. John sends the record-drawing request to the client. First internal check-in is Friday of week one, one page, findings and blockers only.
Progress Review Minutes: Facility Condition Assessment

Progress review held at the {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}} plant conference room to walk the interim deficiency register and agree priorities for the remaining field work. Minutes prepared by John Johnson.
Attendees:

items: Jeff Fowler, Engineering Manager, Torchlake Engineering Group
John Johnson, Project Engineer, Torchlake Engineering Group
Jennifer Mayo, Principal Engineer, Torchlake Engineering Group
David Fowler, Staff Engineer, Torchlake Engineering Group
Megan Thomas, Field Operations Lead, Torchlake Engineering Group

blocks.text: Discussion. John walked the register: 84 findings to date, concentrated in the roof field and the 1970s-era air handlers. David Fowler presented nameplate and runtime data for the mechanical inventory; Jen reviewed his remaining-useful-life calls and tightened two of them, noting the chillers are closer to end of life than the maintenance logs suggest. Ms. Sandoval asked that findings affecting the production line be flagged separately so operations can review them ahead of the final report. Megan Thomas confirmed roof access for the remaining bays is booked.
Decisions. Production-affecting findings get their own register section. The electrical room survey moves ahead of the warehouse to match the client's shutdown window.

rows: Flag production-affecting findings in the register
Book electrical room survey inside shutdown window

blocks.text: Status Report: Facility Condition Assessment

Status: on track. Field work at the {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}} plant is complete, the deficiency register is closed at 112 findings, and the draft report is in internal review. Prepared by Jeff Fowler for the client's leadership.
Completed this period

items: Closed the field register: envelope, structure, mechanical, electrical, and plumbing systems all walked and documented.
Finished the mechanical remaining-useful-life analysis, including the chiller findings reviewed at the progress meeting.
Delivered the production-affecting findings list to operations, per the register split agreed with the client.

blocks.text: Upcoming

items: Draft report to John Johnson for register reconciliation, then to the client for factual review.
Final presentation with the prioritized capital plan and planning-level pricing.

blocks.text: Budget
The engagement remains within the agreed fixed fee of {{fact:f:E-2019-001.fee}}; two of three installments invoiced to date. No scope changes this period.
